title: Why Clean Air Zones Are So Politically Fragile
subtitle: 治好了空气却输了选举：城市减排的政治困局

paragraphs.en: Madrid's low-emission zone was first praised as a public-health success because it reduced dirty-air exposure. After local elections, a new council suspended enforcement and hinted at rollback. Courts later ordered fines to resume, but the policy's future remains politically fragile.
paragraphs.zh: 马德里的低排放区最初因降低了空气污染暴露而被誉为公共健康领域的成功案例。地方选举后，新一届市政当局暂停了执法并暗示可能倒退。尽管法院随后责令恢复罚款，但该政策的未来在政治上依然十分脆弱。

words.meaning: adj. 脆弱的，易被破坏的

paragraphs.en: This reversal highlights a weakness of city-by-city air governance across Europe. Local governments can regulate traffic, but immediate costs fall on drivers who must pay charges or replace vehicles. That cost visibility makes clean-air rules easy campaign targets.
paragraphs.zh: 这一反转凸显了欧洲城市各自为政治理空气污染的结构性弱点。地方政府可以管控交通，但直接成本却落在了必须缴费或更换车辆的司机身上。这种成本的高度可见性，使得清洁空气法规极易成为竞选攻击的目标。

paragraphs.en: Zones like London's ULEZ still deliver measurable health benefits, so they are not symbolic gestures. The deeper failure is that national governments have often been too weak in forcing manufacturers to clean up vehicle emissions upstream. Without stronger national standards, local policies will keep cycling between progress and backlash.
paragraphs.zh: 像伦敦超低排放区这样的区域仍能带来可衡量的健康收益，因此并非象征性姿态。更深层的失败在于，国家政府在迫使车企从源头净化车辆排放方面往往力度过弱。若没有更强有力的国家标准，地方政策只会在进步与反弹之间循环往复。
